title: Images, Layers, Manifests, Tags, Digests, and Registry Identity · Guided Container Studio
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working image identity and layer provenance ledger for images, layers, manifests, tags, digests, and registry identity, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of image identity and layer provenance ledger while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Relate image configuration, root filesystem diff layers, history, chain identity, and a container writable layer.
Distinguish names, repositories, tags, manifest digests, config digests, layer digests, and local image IDs.
Trace registry authentication, manifest-index selection, platform resolution, blob reuse, verification, and unpacking during pull.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Image Config Rootfs Layers
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop release tag moved between two manifest-list digests while linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 resolve to different image configs. Establish immutable identity, layer ownership, registry trust, and pull behavior.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Registry Push Retention
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-registry-push-retention-skw26k". Tailor its image-identity.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Registry Push Retention.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Registry Push Retention into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Registry Push Retention, keep Docker Registry Push Retention connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-registry-push-retention-skw26k
  - competency: docker-registry-push-retention
    artifact: image-identity.yaml
    decision: registry push retention
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: production-like canary with read-only application state
    fault-injection: restore a volume under a different numeric user identity
    retained-proof: attached SBOM provenance signature and policy decision
    case-key: skw26k
    change: |
      reference: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
      manifest: linux-amd64
      config: content-addressed
      layers: ordered-and-shared
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified-digest
    failure: Reject a mutable tag as release identity or a history listing as proof that secrets never entered a layer.
    repair: Resolve the digest, inspect manifest and config metadata, trace layer ownership, and retain provenance for the selected platform.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Docker Registry Push Retention structural check is only one gate. image identity and layer provenance ledger must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
